paper.title: 591 high velocity stars in the Galactic halo selected from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2

extraction.summary: Partial extraction: paper reports 591 HiVelSCs, with at least 43 having unbound probability >50% under the most conservative potential model and up to 304 under the least conservative model. The LaTeX source only prints the 20 fastest candidates in detail (Tables 1,2,6,7,8), which are all among the possibly unbound set. The remaining candidates are only available in an external CSV at http://paperdata.china-vo.org/LYB/lamostdr7_gaiadr2_hvs_591.csv, which cannot be accessed within this pipeline. Therefore, only the 20 printed HiVelSCs (Hivel1–Hivel20) are extracted as possibly unbound stars based on the published tables and explicit discussion of their unbound probabilities.

method_chain.summary: Cross-match LAMOST DR7 spectra with Gaia DR2 within 5 arcsec using TOPCAT to build LAMOST-Gaia catalog of >10 million entries.

source_refs.context: cross-match of LAMOST DR7 with Gaia DR2 were performed with a radius of 5 arc sec, and outputs a catalogue (LAMOST-Gaia hereafter) consisting of over 10 million entries

method_chain.summary: Select stars with positive parallax, fractional parallax error f_var <= 0.2, and either escaping under at least one of seven Galactic potentials or V_GC >= 450 km/s, reducing to 31,440 entries. After inspection of spectra for RV reliability, 591 HiVelSCs are retained.

source_refs.context: selection of low-f sample and then escaping/bound criteria

method_chain.summary: Inspect spectra to retain only those with reliable RV, select highest S/N for stars with multiple observations. Final 591 HiVelSCs.

source_refs.context: check the spectral qualities and only retain spectra with the highest signal to noise ratios

method_chain.summary: Adopt LASP radial velocities, apply zero-point correction of -5.4 km/s using Huang+2018 RV standard stars.

source_refs.context: adopt RV_LASP as final RVs and zero-point correction of -5.4 km/s

method_chain.summary: Use Gaia DR2 astrometric parameters (parallax, proper motions, positions) with full covariance matrix and correlations.

source_refs.context: five astrometric parameters in Gaia DR2 catalogue... multivariate Gaussian with covariance matrix

method_chain.summary: Invert parallax to obtain heliocentric distance; apply MC sampling (1000 realizations) to propagate uncertainties to r_GC and V_GC.

source_refs.context: 1000 MC samplings ... 1000 r_GC and V_GC can be obtained

method_chain.summary: Adopt solar Galactocentric distance R0=8.2 kpc, solar offset from disk z_sun=25 pc, LSR circular velocity 238 km/s, and solar peculiar motion (U,V,W)=(14.0, 12.24, 7.25) km/s.

source_refs.context: d_odot = 8.2 kpc, z_sun = 25 pc, LSR motion 238 km/s, solar peculiar motion (14.0, 12.24, 7.25) km/s

method_chain.summary: Compute Galactocentric Cartesian coordinates (x,y,z) and velocities (Vx,Vy,Vz), total velocity V_GC from the MC distributions using the assumed solar parameters and corrected RVs.

source_refs.context: The Galactic Cartesian coordinate system ... We use the median (50th percentile) and the 16th and 84th percentiles

method_chain.summary: Adopt seven Galactic potential models: Paczynski+1990, Gnedin+2005, Xue+2008, Koposov+2010, Kenyon+2014, MWPotential2014, Watkins+2019. Compute escape velocity at each star's position.

parameters.name: potential_name
parameters.raw_value: Watkins+2019, Gnedin+2005, Kenyon+2014, Koposov+2010, Paczynski+1990, MWPotential2014, Xue+2008
parameters.value: Watkins+2019, Gnedin+2005, Kenyon+2014, Koposov+2010, Paczynski+1990, MWPotential2014, Xue+2008

source_refs.context: Seven Galactic potential models ... are adopted here to estimate the escape velocities

method_chain.summary: Compute unbound probability Pub as fraction of 1000 MC realizations with V_GC > V_esc, and also compute Pgc (fraction with rmin<1 kpc) and PMW (rmin<25 kpc).

source_refs.context: estimate the probability Pub of being unbound from the Milky Way... defined as the percentage of V_GC larger than escape velocity

method_chain.summary: Trace orbits back 10 Gyr using MWPotential2014 in Galpy, 1000 MC realizations, compute eccentricity e, Zmax, rmin, and orbital energy difference E-Phi(inf).

source_refs.context: perform numerical orbit integrations ... using the python package Galpy ... MWPotential2014

method_chain.summary: Classify HiVelSCs into HVS, HRS, RS, OUT based on thresholds: HVS if Pgc > 0.16; HRS if Pgc<0.16, PMW>0.5, Pub>0.5; RS if Pgc<0.16, PMW>0.5, Pub<0.5; OUT if Pgc<0.16, PMW<0.5.

parameters.name: other
parameters.raw_value: HVS: Pgc>0.16; HRS: Pgc<0.16, PMW>0.5, Pub>0.5; RS: Pgc<0.16, PMW>0.5, Pub<0.5; OUT: Pgc<0.16, PMW<0.5

source_refs.context: classified criteria ... HVS candidates: Pgc > 0.16 ...

method_chain.summary: Determine atmospheric parameters Teff, log g, [Fe/H] from LASP and DD-Payne pipelines, and [α/Fe] from DD-Payne.

source_refs.context: method of data-driven Payne (DD-Payne) ... also provides atmospheric parameters ... LASP atmospheric parameters

inclusion_assessment.summary: Hivel1 is one of the 591 HiVelSCs and the 20 fastest, with unbound probability >99.6% in the most conservative potential model (Watkins+2019).
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Unbound probability explicitly listed in Table 7 (Pub(W)=0.996); paper states that stars with Pub>50% are possibly unbound.

source_refs.context: at least 43 HiVelSCs have Pub > 50% of being unbound ... Table 7 lists these probabilities for the 20 fastest HiVelSCs
Table caption: Unbound probabilities of the 20 fastest HiVelSCs ... Hivel1 Pub(W)=0.996

candidate_groups_considered.description: The 591 HiVelSCs selected from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2, of which up to 304 are possibly unbound under the least conservative potential model.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Only the 20 fastest candidates are published in printed tables; the remaining 571 candidates are only available in an external CSV file that cannot be accessed by this pipeline, so they cannot be individually extracted.

source_refs.context: A catalog for these 591 HiVelSCs, ... is available at http://paperdata.china-vo.org/LYB/lamostdr7_gaiadr2_hvs_591.csv
We construct a catalogue including 93 columns to list various parameters for these 591 HiVelSCs

candidate_groups_considered.description: The up to 304 HiVelSCs with Pub > 50% under Xue+2008 potential (including the 43 definitely unbound under Watkins+2019).
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Identifiers and data for these objects are only in the external CSV; cannot be extracted from the provided sources.

source_refs.context: at most 304 HiVelSCs have Pub > 50% of being unbound in Xue+2008 potential model
